docs.title: local-terminal-llm
docs.text:
ask is a local-first terminal workflow built around the llm CLI, optional local retrieval, and shell-native command execution.

This site documents how to install it, configure it, and use its higher-level features safely.

docs.title: What You Get
docs.text:
• A Python CLI (askllm) normally invoked as ask
  • Normal Q&A mode with selectable models
  • Command-only mode (-co) with optional direct execution (-no)
  • Retrieval grounding:
  • --web for current web context
  • --docs for local document collections
  • --auto for heuristic routing
  • Text-to-speech with Piper (--tts) in full or stream mode
  • Shell integration for bash, zsh, fish, and similar shells
  • Configurable prompt-insert hotkeys for ask, codex, claude, or custom commands

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
./scripts/setup.sh
ask "Explain rsync -avz"

Only install/validate dependencies:

./scripts/setup.sh --dep-only

docs.title: Typical Flows
docs.text:
# command generation only
ask -co "find the 20 largest files"

# command generation + execute after confirmation
ask -co -no "rename all .jpeg files to .jpg in this repo"

# stream speech while tokens are generated
ask --tts --tts-configure stream "summarize this changelog"

docs.title: Documentation Structure
docs.text:
• Motivation
  • Installation
  • Quickstart
  • Guides
  • Configuration Reference
  • Safety and Security
  • Troubleshooting

docs.title: Local Validation
docs.text:
Run quick checks:

python3 -m py_compile askllm scripts/devdocs_scraper.py
./askllm --help
./scripts/setup.sh --help

docs.title: Documentation Build
docs.text:
mkdocs build -f docs-site/mkdocs.yml --strict

Serve locally:

mkdocs serve -f docs-site/mkdocs.yml

docs.title: GitHub Pages Deployment
docs.text:
Deployment is automated via .github/workflows/docs-site-pages.yml.

On push to main (or manual dispatch), the workflow:

  1. installs MkDocs and Material theme dependencies
  2. builds docs-site
  3. uploads artifact
  4. deploys to GitHub Pages

docs.title: Repository Practices
docs.text:
• Keep scripts shell-agnostic where possible.
  • Prefer explicit flags over implicit behavior.
  • Keep command execution guarded and confirmable.

docs.title: Problem
docs.text:
Terminal users often need fast model assistance without leaving the shell and without losing control over what actually runs on their machine.

Two common pain points:

  • Generic chat UX does not map cleanly to shell workflows.
  • Command generation can be unsafe unless execution is explicit and inspectable.

docs.title: 1. Local-first by default
docs.text: ask is designed to work with local model backends through llm and tools like Ollama.

docs.title: 2. Shell-native ergonomics
docs.text: The tool is installed as a normal CLI command and integrates with shell aliases and hotkeys.

docs.title: 3. Explicit safety boundaries
docs.text:
• -co generates commands only.
  • -no executes generated commands only after a confirmation prompt.

docs.title: 4. Better answers when needed
docs.text: The wrapper can inject retrieval context from web or local docs before model invocation.

docs.title: 5. Practical speed and feedback
docs.text: Streaming output, spinner styles, and optional streaming TTS keep interaction responsive.

docs.title: Why This Approach
docs.text:
This project favors practical terminal productivity over heavy framework complexity:

  • minimal dependencies
  • inspectable scripts
  • deterministic shell setup
  • explicit flags for powerful behavior

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Required:

  • Linux/macOS shell environment (zsh, bash, fish, etc.)
  • Python 3.11+ (for the CLI script)
  • llm installed and available on PATH

Optional but recommended:

  • Ollama (or another configured llm provider)
  • piper and aplay for text-to-speech
  • ddgr or Exa API key for web retrieval modes

docs.title: One-command Setup
docs.text:
./scripts/setup.sh

The setup script can:

  • install askllm
  • append shell extension sourcing to the right shell config file
  • configure alias and hotkeys
  • validate/install runtime dependencies (including llm)

During interactive setup, you will be asked whether to use the default alias ask. Press Enter for default, or choose custom alias input.

After setup, you can configure all environment keys with:

ask --env-setup

docs.title: Dependency-only Mode
docs.text:
Use this when you only want dependency validation/install checks:

./scripts/setup.sh --dep-only

docs.title: Manual Installation
docs.text:
Install the CLI only:

./scripts/install-askllm.sh

Install shell extension only:

./scripts/install-shell-extension.sh --shell zsh
./scripts/install-shell-extension.sh --shell bash
./scripts/install-shell-extension.sh --shell fish

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
ask --help
./scripts/setup.sh --help

If ask is not found, reload your shell config or open a new shell session.

docs.title: Basic Prompt
docs.text: ask "Explain the difference between rsync -avz and -a"

docs.title: Command-only Generation
docs.text: ask -co "find files larger than 1GB"

docs.title: Execute Generated Command with Confirmation
docs.text: ask -co -no "delete all .tmp files older than 7 days in /var/tmp"

docs.title: Retrieval Modes
docs.text:
ask --web "latest bun install instructions"
ask --docs mydocs "how do vite path aliases work"
ask --auto "latest react router api changes"

docs.title: Model Selection
docs.text:
# one-time override
ask -m llama3.2:3b "summarize this"

# persist default model
ask -ms
ask -ms llama3.2:3b

docs.title: Environment Setup
docs.text:
# prompt for all supported env keys and provider keys
ask --env-setup

docs.title: TTS
docs.text:
# speak after full output
ask --tts "summarize this log"

# stream speech while generating
ask --tts --tts-configure stream "read this aloud while generating"

docs.title: Generate Commands Only
docs.text:
ask -co "find all jpeg files modified in last 24 hours"

-co keeps model output constrained to shell command generation.

docs.title: Execute with Confirmation
docs.text:
ask -co -no "rename all .jpeg files to .jpg recursively"

Behavior:

  1. Generate command
  2. Show confirmation prompt
  3. Execute only if you confirm

docs.title: Safety Recommendations
docs.text:
• Prefer reviewing generated commands before execution.
  • Use narrower prompts that mention exact paths/scopes.
  • Avoid broad destructive prompts without path constraints.

docs.title: Command Format
docs.text:
ask [flags] "prompt"

You can also pipe context through stdin:

cat package.json | ask "summarize dependencies"

docs.title: Main Modes
docs.text:
• Standard response mode (default)
  • Command-only mode (-co)
  • Retrieval-augmented mode (--web, --docs, --auto)

docs.title: System Prompt Control
docs.text: ask -s "be concise and output bullet points" "explain namespaces in C++"

docs.title: Spinner Controls
docs.text:
ask --spinner-style dots "explain rsync"
ask --spinner-style-select bounce
ask --no-spinner "quick answer"

docs.title: Escaping and Quoting Prompts
docs.text:
Use quotes for prompts with shell wildcards:

ask --web "what day is it?"

Without quoting in shells like zsh, ? and * may be glob-expanded before ask receives your prompt.

docs.title: DevDocs Scraper
docs.text: scripts/devdocs_scraper.py can list top-level DevDocs docsets, let you select interactively, scrape documents, and run embeddings via llm embed-multi.

docs.title: List Available Top-level Docsets
docs.text: ./scripts/devdocs_scraper.py --list-only

docs.title: Interactive Selection
docs.text:
./scripts/devdocs_scraper.py

Interactive selector behavior:

  • top [ ] Select all option
  • [X] marker for selected entries
  • terminal-driven multi-select flow

docs.title: Non-interactive Selection
docs.text:
./scripts/devdocs_scraper.py --select react,python
./scripts/devdocs_scraper.py --select all

docs.title: Embedding Output
docs.text:
Scraped JSONL is written under:

  • ~/.local/share/ask/processed/<docset>.jsonl

Embedding command shape:

llm embed-multi <docset>_docs ~/.local/share/ask/processed/<docset>.jsonl \
  -d ~/.local/share/ask/docs.db \
  -m sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 \
  --store

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
./scripts/devdocs_scraper.py --help

Useful flags:

  • --embedding-model
  • --embed-db
  • --processed-dir
  • --no-embed
  • --max-pages

docs.title: Shell Extension
docs.text:
The installer appends shellExtension.sh (or fish equivalent) to your shell config.

./scripts/install-shell-extension.sh --shell zsh
./scripts/install-shell-extension.sh --shell fish

docs.title: Default Alias
docs.text: Default alias is ask, configurable during setup or via --alias.

docs.title: Hotkey Setup via Script
docs.text:
Hotkeys are disabled by default when using setup.sh.

./scripts/setup.sh --shell zsh --hotkey '^G'
./scripts/setup.sh --shell zsh --codex-hotkey '^O' --claude-hotkey '^Y'

Remove all existing askllm keybindings:

./scripts/remove-keybindings.sh

If your current Bash session still has TAB remapped, run:

bind '"\C-i": complete'

docs.title: Environment Variable Controls
docs.text:
• ASK_HOTKEY_ASK
  • ASK_HOTKEY_ASK_CMD
  • ASK_HOTKEY_CODEX
  • ASK_HOTKEY_CODEX_CMD
  • ASK_HOTKEY_CLAUDE
  • ASK_HOTKEY_CLAUDE_CMD
  • ASK_HOTKEY_CUSTOM
  • ASK_HOTKEY_CUSTOM_CMD

Example:

export ASK_HOTKEY_ASK='^G'
export ASK_HOTKEY_ASK_CMD='ask '

docs.title: One-time Model Override
docs.text: ask -m llama3.2:3b "summarize this"

docs.title: Persist Default Model
docs.text:
Both flags are equivalent:

ask -ms
ask -sm

Direct persistence without the picker:

ask -ms llama3.2:3b

Stored in:

  • ~/.config/askllm/config.json (unless ASK_CONFIG_FILE is set)

docs.title: Power Model Flow (OpenAI)
docs.text:
ask -pc gpt-5.4
ask -p "analyze this architecture and provide migration phases"
  • -pc sets a dedicated power model
  • -p uses the configured power model for current invocation

docs.title: Missing API Key Handling
docs.text:
If a selected provider key is missing, the CLI can prompt you to paste a key and retry. You can also preconfigure all provider/env keys with:

ask --env-setup

docs.title: Web Grounding
docs.text:
ask --web "latest python release highlights"
  • Uses wrapper-controlled retrieval provider logic
  • Intended for freshness-sensitive prompts

Provider selection:

  • --web-provider auto
  • --web-provider exa
  • --web-provider ddgr

docs.title: Local Docs Grounding
docs.text:
ask --docs react_docs "how to use useMemo correctly"

Documents root defaults to ~/.local/share/ask/docs and can be changed via --docs-db or ASK_DOCS_DB.

docs.title: Auto Routing
docs.text:
ask --auto "latest bun install process"

Heuristic behavior:

  • freshness-sensitive prompts prefer web retrieval
  • otherwise docs retrieval if available
  • fallback to ungrounded if no backend is available

docs.title: Enable TTS
docs.text: ask --tts "summarize this"

docs.title: Full mode
docs.text:
Speaks after full model response is complete:

ask --tts --tts-configure full "explain this"

docs.title: Stream mode
docs.text:
Speaks while tokens are being generated:

ask --tts --tts-configure stream "read this while generating"

docs.title: Runtime Requirements
docs.text:
• piper executable on PATH
  • aplay executable on PATH
  • valid Piper model file

Default model path:

  • ~/.local/share/piper/en_US-lessac-medium.onnx

docs.title: Relevant Environment Variables
docs.text:
• ASK_TTS_MODEL
  • ASK_TTS_SAMPLE_RATE
  • ASK_TTS_MODE
  • ASK_TTS_DELAY_SECONDS (buffer delay, clamped to ~100-300ms)

docs.title: Implementation Notes
docs.text:
Current streaming behavior includes:

  • small delay buffer for smoother cadence
  • tiny chunk merging to reduce choppiness
  • flush on Piper stdin after each chunk
  • Ctrl+C interrupt support for immediate stop
  • lock-based protection against overlapping audio playback

docs.title: CLI Flags
docs.text:
Run full help:

ask --help

High-impact flags:

  • -co, --command-only
  • -no, --no-output
  • -m, --model
  • -sm, --select-model
  • -ms, --model-select
  • -pc, --power-config
  • -p, --power
  • --web, --docs, --auto
  • --tts, --tts-configure
  • --env-setup, --env-shell, --env-config-file

docs.title: Interactive Env Setup
docs.text:
Configure all supported environment keys in one guided flow:

ask --env-setup

Optional shell/config targeting:

ask --env-setup --env-shell zsh --env-config-file ~/.zshrc

By default, API keys entered in this setup are stored in your local llm key store and are not written to shell config unless you explicitly opt in.

docs.title: Config File
docs.text:
Default:

  • ~/.config/askllm/config.json

May contain keys like:

  • default_model
  • power_model
  • spinner_style

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Core:

  • ASK_MODEL
  • ASK_POWER_MODEL
  • ASK_SYSTEM
  • ASK_CONFIG_FILE
  • ASK_ALIAS_NAME
  • ASKLLM_BIN

Retrieval:

  • ASK_DOCS_DB
  • ASK_DOCS_COLLECTION
  • ASK_WEB_PROVIDER

UI:

  • ASK_SPINNER_STYLE

TTS:

  • ASK_TTS_MODEL
  • ASK_TTS_SAMPLE_RATE
  • ASK_TTS_MODE
  • ASK_TTS_DELAY_SECONDS

Hotkeys:

  • ASK_HOTKEY_ASK
  • ASK_HOTKEY_ASK_CMD
  • ASK_HOTKEY_CODEX
  • ASK_HOTKEY_CODEX_CMD
  • ASK_HOTKEY_CLAUDE
  • ASK_HOTKEY_CLAUDE_CMD
  • ASK_HOTKEY_CUSTOM
  • ASK_HOTKEY_CUSTOM_CMD

Provider API key env names:

  • OPENAI_API_KEY
  • ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
  • GOOGLE_API_KEY
  • MISTRAL_API_KEY
  • GROQ_API_KEY
  • OPENROUTER_API_KEY
  • COHERE_API_KEY
  • HUGGINGFACE_API_KEY
  • EXA_API_KEY

docs.title: Key Storage Model
docs.text:
• API keys entered through ask setup flows are stored locally using llm keys set.
  • This key store is offline/local to your machine and is not synced by this project.
  • Key read/write operations in ask use the local llm package workflow (llm keys set/get).

docs.title: Shell Config Behavior
docs.text:
• ask --env-setup writes non-secret environment values into a marked block in your shell config.
  • API keys are not written into shell config by default.
  • You can explicitly opt in to writing API keys as plain environment variables, but that is less secure.

docs.title: Runtime Behavior
docs.text:
• ask runs locally and invokes local CLI tools (llm, optional ddgr, optional piper/aplay).
  • Retrieval/API requests happen only when you invoke features that require them (for example --web with Exa).
  • Key material is used only for the provider calls you trigger in the current workflow.

docs.title: zsh: no matches found: ...?
docs.text:
Cause: unquoted wildcard characters (?, *, [) in prompt text.

Fix:

ask --web "what day is it?"

If shell extension is installed for zsh, it may also wrap ask with noglob for convenience.

docs.title: Unknown embedding model in llm embed-multi
docs.text:
Example:

Unknown model: sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2

Cause: embedding model/plugin alias not registered in your llm environment.

Checks:

llm embed-models
llm plugins

Use a model identifier available in your local llm installation or install the required embedding plugin/provider.

docs.title: Unknown chat model in ask
docs.text:
Example:

Error: 'Unknown model: phi3:mini'

If run in an interactive terminal, ask now prompts:

Model 'phi3:mini' is not installed. Install it now? [Y/n]

On Y, it attempts local model setup (llm-ollama provider + ollama pull <model>), then retries. If setup still fails, verify:

llm plugins
ollama list

docs.title: TTS not speaking
docs.text:
Check:

which piper
which aplay

Ensure the model path exists:

echo "$ASK_TTS_MODEL"

Or set it explicitly:

export ASK_TTS_MODEL="$HOME/.local/share/piper/en_US-lessac-medium.onnx"

docs.title: ask command not found
docs.text:
• Open a new terminal session
  • Ensure shell config has extension/source block
  • Confirm install path is in PATH
  • Re-run ./scripts/setup.sh to reinstall dependencies and shell wiring

docs.title: llm is not installed or not on PATH
docs.text:
./scripts/setup.sh

Or dependency-only mode:

./scripts/setup.sh --dep-only

docs.title: Web retrieval unavailable
docs.text:
• Verify selected provider dependencies
  • Verify API keys for external providers if used
